action: LEAK CHECK GOOD

problem: Crank case right above accesory case leaking
action: Applied high temp silicone to the aft top part of crankcase where accesory case meets the cylinder case halves. Leak check good.

problem: Crew stated ALT fail annunciator CAS message every 20-30 sec. ALT amps always 8-14 and always around 28 volts. battery always 0.
action: Removed and replaced ALT INOP switch, ALT fuse holder, and ALT AUX wire from ALT to firewall with new. Performed ground run and ran aircraft in multiple configurations with no ALT fail annunciator CAS messages.

problem: At power idle the engine idles at 740 RPM. After landing at GFK went idle, airplane started to shake hard. The engine almost cut out at idle.
action: Ran A/C was able to duplicate discrep at lower RPM. Removed prop and rotated prop 180 degress. Reinstalled prop and ran A/C discrep still occured. Removed prop and started transfering bulkhead and spacers to overhauled prop. Torque checked engine mount bolts to engine and firewall no defects noted.

problem: LH engine alternator wiring worn
action: Spliced LH engine F1 and aux wires at firewall and ran new wiring to alternator. Installed new fuse terminal due to old one breaking.

problem: RH ENG alternator wire worn
action: Spliced new F1 wire section from firewall to alternator.

problem: R/H Magneto External Timing Out-of-Spec
action: Retimed R/H Magneto to Engine, Removed and Replaced Magneto Base Gasket and Crankcase Adaptor Gasket

problem: during flight, we noticed warm air coming from under panel even when heat/defrost controls were selected off.
action: inspected, found cable bent at heater lever in cabin,removed copilot seat, repositioned cable, adjusted valve linkages on firewall and tightened swivel hardware. cable ended up breaking on lever in cabin. ordered new cable

problem: "L ALT FAIL" CAS message when left engine is above 1200 RPM. Goes away when eng is idle. Ran single alt fail check, did not resolve. Left alternator was indicating 10 amps.
action: Removed front seats.  Inspected wiring from P501 and P502 regulator connectors to firewall J719 connector.    Disassembled GIA1 1P605 and GIA2 2P605 connectors and removed racks for access.   Fabricated new K24B22 wire for L ALT INOP indication.

problem: "L ALT FAIL" CAS message when left engine is above 1200 RPM. Goes away when eng is idle. Ran single alt fail check, did not resolve. Left alternator was indicating 10 amps.
action: Routed new K24B22 wire between J719, 1p605 and 2p605.   Terminated ends and reassembled connectors, racks and reinstalled front seats.    Retied harnesses.  Checked wiring at F102 on Left alternator and resoldered fuse holder with new wire to aux terminal and P3B20 to firewall.   Ran aircraft and operation tests good.

problem: OIL LEAKING ON TO EXHAUST SHROUD. FORMING DROPS AND DRIPPING ON GROUND
action: WASHED ENGINE. RAN A/C AND FOUND ACTIVE OIL DRIP NEAR CRANKSHAFT SEAL.

problem: OIL LEAKING ON TO EXHAUST SHROUD. FORMING DROPS AND DRIPPING ON GROUND
action: REMOVED LOWER SHEAVE AND FAN WHEEL. REMOVED AND REPLACED CRANKSHAFT SEAL WITH NEW.

problem: Alt amps showed yellow x on display after start up, would show 14 amps the x out periodically. Battery amps showed 0.
action: Removed alternator shunt from firewall, inspected alternator shunt, found the wire for the 103 fuse on alternator shunt solder to be loose. Resoldered wire for alternator shunt to 103 fuse. Reinstalled alternator shunt to firewall. Ran aircraft, ops check good

problem: Erroneous fuel flow reading between 0.0-1.2 after startup
action: Inspected fuel flow transducer wiring to firewall and cannon plugs, no defects noted. Cleaned cannon plugs and applied electrical compound to pins. Ran aircraft, ops check good.

problem: Alternator control relay wire damaged on EIS install.
action: Cut out damaged alternator control relay wire where it was damaged near the firewall and spliced the wires back together.

problem: Engine has 3,113.1 hours TSO; 5,568.5 hours TT
action: Removed engine, S/N L-36411-51E. Cleaned and inspected engine mount and firewall. Installed overhauled engine P/N IO-360-B4A. S/N L-38893-51E, using new shock mounts, bolts, and serviceable locking nuts. Torqued to spec. REF M.M. CH 71-20. Installed new fuel and oil hoses.Installed overhauled fuel servo P/N AV2581600 S/N AV249453. Connected fuel hoses, throttle and mixture controls and safetied hardware.

problem: A595-1 Vertical firewall seal due for time change
action: removed and replaced vertical firewall seal with new.

problem: A595-2 Vertical firewall seal due for time change
action: removed and replaced vertical firewall seal with new.

problem: A595-1 Vertical firewall seal due for time change
action: Assisted

problem: A595-2 Vertical firewall seal due for time change
action: Assisted

problem: Firewall electric fuel pump is inoperative.
action: Inspected electric fuel pump and electrical connections. Measured 28 volts at connector. Removed electric fuel pump S/N 243334, cleaned and transferred fittings with new o-rings to overhauled fuel pump P/N 18030-B S/N 222269. Installed fuel pump. Ops and leak checked good.

problem: Engine has 3,577.2 hours TSO; 9,182.5 hours TT
action: Assisted with engine removal, cleaned and prepared mounts, firewall. Installed overhauled engine S/N L-14983-51E, torqued mounting hardware to spec

problem: Engine timed out
action: Removed engine from aircraft, cleaned and inspected firewall and engine mount no defects noted

problem: Oil leak from top seam of right engine crankcase.
action: Applied sealant to top seam of RH engine crankcase. Applied RTV in top 1/4" thru-bolts.

problem: Oil leak from top seam of right engine crankcase.
action: Assisted

problem: Oil leak from top seam of right engine crankcase.
action: Leak check good.

problem: CRANKSHAFT SEAL SPINNING WITH CRANK
action: REMOVED PROP, REMOVED OLD CRANKSHAFT SEAL, STARTED CLEANING

problem: CRANKSHAFT SEAL SPINNING WITH CRANK
action: CLEANED CRANKSHAFT SEAL AREA, PREPPED SEAL AREA, INSTALLED CRANKSHAFT SEAL OVER CRANKSHAFT, INSTALLED SEAL USING PLIOBOND 20, REINSTALLED PROP, TORQUED AND SAFETIED PROP, SET ALTERNATOR BELT TENSION, LEAK CHECK REQUIRED

problem: CRANKSHAFT SEAL SPINNING WITH CRANK
action: OPS AND LEAK CHECK GOOD

problem: Fuel servo nuts loose.
action: Disconnected fuel hose to firewall at servo. Tightened and torqued nuts to spec. Reconnected fuel hose. Leak check good.
